title: OS Command Injection Detected: Critical System File Access Attempt

steps.message: Immediately block the identified attacker IP and payload patterns used for OS command injection, as prevention involves 'input ﬁ lters, 374–377' and 'preventing, 367–368' such attacks.

Isolate the affected host to prevent further compromise and contain the attack, as OS command execution allows attackers to 'compromise of the application, enabling you to access data belonging to other users, perform unauthorized actions, and inject arbitrary code and commands'.

Query SIEM for all logs related to the affected application and host for the last 24 hours, specifically looking for the payload "; cat /etc/passwd" or other 'shell metacharacters, 363, 365' and 'scripting error messages that may indicate that your input is being executed'. This helps 'detecting, 372–374' and understand the 'causes, 368–369' of the injection.
steps.target: index=* source={{application_log}} host={{affected_host_id}} ("; cat /etc/passwd" OR "Runtime.exec" OR "scripting error")

steps.message: Review the SIEM analysis and logs to confirm the vulnerability. Authorize development of a code fix to properly sanitize user input and prevent 'java.lang.runtime.Runtime.exec' from accepting arbitrary shell metacharacters, implementing 'input ﬁ lters, 374–377' to prevent 'OS command execution, 738'.

Deploy the approved code patch to remediate the OS command injection vulnerability. Additionally, reset all application-related credentials, especially if 'Password files for the operating system and application' could have been accessed.

Notify the incident response team and stakeholders about the successful containment and remediation of the OS command injection incident, summarizing the steps taken to 'preventing, 367–368' recurrence.

params.message: RCE Incident PB-RCE-001: OS Command Injection via shell metacharacters has been contained and remediated. Affected host isolated, WAF rules deployed, code patched, and credentials reset. Further monitoring in place.
